ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 represents green tea beverage as a category; no Ayataka-specific entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers green tea as a beverage category. No brand-specific Ayataka code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 refers to green tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this bottled beverage product.

main_ingredients: green tea leaves (gyokuro and sencha blend)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Ayataka Green Tea bottle contains 525 ml, which is the typical single-serve portion sold in Singapore convenience stores and restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, green, ready-to-drink, unsweetened
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Foundation Foods / SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka Green Tea is a Japanese commercially bottled unsweetened green tea (Coca-Cola Japan). No direct USDA branded entry for Ayataka was available. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for unsweetened ready-to-drink green tea (FDC ID 171917), which closely matches the product formulation. Ayataka is brewed from gyokuro and sencha leaves with no added sugar, consistent with the USDA unsweetened green tea profile. Per-100g values reflect the beverage as consumed.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — plain unsweetened green tea
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain unsweetened green tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates and has an effectively zero glycemic response. No direct GI test for Ayataka brand exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. By analogy with plain brewed green tea and other unsweetened tea beverages, GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value per Veritas convention) to indicate effectively zero glycemic impact. Glycemic load per 525 ml serving is 0.0 given carbohydrate_g of 0.2 per 100g (1.05g per serving), yielding GL = (1 × 1.05) / 100 = 0.01, rounded to 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (1 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea contains approximately 1 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it a sodium-safe beverage for individuals managing hypertension; regular green tea consumption has also been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and zero added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea is unsweetened with approximately 0.2 g carbohydrate per 100 ml and no added sugar, resulting in a negligible glycemic load; it is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and may support glycemic control through catechin-mediated insulin sensitivity effects documented in peer-reviewed literature.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of catechins
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea contains no fat, no saturated fat, and no cholesterol, and provides catechins (particularly EGCG) that have been associated with LDL cholesterol reduction and endothelial function improvement in clinical studies, supporting its suitability for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka Green Tea (綾鷹 緑茶) is a commercially bottled, ready-to-drink Japanese green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan. It is brewed from a proprietary blend of gyokuro and sencha green tea leaves, giving it a characteristically smooth, slightly umami-rich, and mildly astringent flavour profile that distinguishes it from typical mass-market bottled teas. The beverage contains no added sugar, no artificial sweeteners, and no preservatives, making it one of the cleaner bottled tea options available in the Asian market. It is sold in 525 ml PET bottles and is widely distributed across Japan, Singapore, and other Asian markets through convenience stores, supermarkets, and food delivery platforms. Nutritionally, Ayataka Green Tea is essentially calorie-free at approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrate content. It contains trace amounts of potassium and iron derived from the tea leaves. The beverage is rich in polyphenolic catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which have been studied for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and metabolic health benefits. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible glycemic load, very low sodium content, and absence of dietary fat. It is suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free dietary patterns.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Ayataka green tea?
Is Ayataka green tea sugar free?
Can diabetics drink Ayataka green tea?
What is Ayataka green tea made from?
Is Ayataka green tea suitable for weight loss?
Does Ayataka green tea contain caffeine?

confidence_reason: Ayataka Green Tea is a well-known commercial product with a consistent formulation. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for unsweetened ready-to-drink green tea, which closely matches the product profile. No direct USDA Branded Foods entry for Ayataka was confirmed. GI is estimated by analogy with plain unsweetened green tea from the University of Sydney GI Database convention. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact branded-product nutrient entry from a primary approved source was available.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100g; this is a trace caloric value consistent with unsweetened brewed tea and is not zero. 2. protein_g is naturally zero as unsweetened green tea contains no protein by nature. 3. fat_g is naturally zero as unsweetened green tea contains no fat by nature. Confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917). 4. sugar_g is confirmed zero; Ayataka is unsweetened with no added sugar. 5. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero for a liquid tea beverage with no particulate matter. 6. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for zero-GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate per serving is negligible. 7. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this beverage. 8. Multilingual naming: Japanese name 綾鷹 緑茶 is mandatory and present. Chinese names (simplified and traditional) are included as the product is widely sold in Chinese-speaking markets. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable as this is a Japanese commercial product, not a Singaporean dish. 9. Ontology codes are real and defensible; no codes were invented. FoodOn FOODON_03301103, FoodEx2 A00LC, and SNOMED CT 16313001 are used as closest parent matches with gap notes. 10. Price data from field survey data (SGD 2.50) is preserved as contextual metadata in the source record but is not included in the nutritional output as it is not a nutritional field.

improvement_actions_taken: Set glycemic_index to 1 per Veritas zero-calorie beverage convention (not null or 0).
Set gi_category to 'low' consistent with GI = 1 (≤ 55 threshold).
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g and fat_g (naturally zero).
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source-not-reported reasons.
Verified aliases_by_language entries are all present verbatim in flat aliases list.
Used only closest_parent match_type values for ontology (no invented match types).
Cuisine correctly set to Japanese; mandatory Japanese local name 綾鷹 緑茶 included.
